name: Band Straight-Arm Pulldown
resistance_profile: Band anchored overhead; with near-straight arms the lats extend the shoulder against tension that peaks at the bottom, biasing the shortened, fully contracted position.
cues: Hold a fixed slight elbow bend throughout
Draw the hands to the thighs using the armpits
Squeeze the lats at the bottom
execution_steps: Anchor a band overhead and stand facing it, holding the band with both hands and a slight forward lean.
Set a small fixed bend in the elbows and keep it locked there for the whole set.
Keeping the arms nearly straight, pull the band down in an arc to the front of the thighs using the lats.
Squeeze the lats hard at the bottom with the hands beside the hips.
Let the arms rise back overhead under control to a full lat stretch.
good_when: You want to isolate the lats with minimal elbow-flexor involvement.
Training at home with only a band.
You want a strong peak contraction to finish off back work.
bad_when: You want a heavy compound back builder.
You cannot keep the elbows from bending as you fatigue.
common_errors: Bending the elbows and turning it into a triceps pushdown
Using torso momentum to move the band
progressions: Use a thicker band or add a pause at the bottom contraction.
Progress to a cable straight-arm pulldown for more constant tension.
regressions: Use a lighter band or work one arm at a time.
Reduce the range slightly if the overhead stretch is uncomfortable.
